briefdescription.para: FMC selection: 0 = old FMC, 1 = new FMC.

detaileddescription.para:
This function demonstrates a complete VISP SS application workflow:
Initialize memory management system
Perform hardware reset sequence
Setup interrupt system
Configure VISP SS subsystem
Initialize ISP to RPU mapping
Setup frame buffer writers (if available)
Initialize mailbox communication
Run the main video benchmark application
Cleanup resources

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative value on error
The function follows Xilinx recommended initialization sequence
Memory manager must be initialized before any buffer allocations

briefdescription.para: Configure VISP SS (Video Image Signal Processing Subsystem)

detaileddescription.para: This function performs the complete initialization and configuration of the VISP SS driver. The VISP SS is the main video processing pipeline that handles camera input, ISP processing, and video output formatting.

Configuration steps:
Lookup device configuration based on device ID or base address
Initialize the driver with the configuration
Verify single-stream operation (non-MCM mode)

parameterdescription.para: - Base address of the VISP SS hardware block

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration XST_FAILURE on configuration lookup or initialization failure XST_INVALID_VERSION if MCM mode is detected (not supported)
This example only supports non-MCM (Multi-Channel Mode) operation
The function uses either device ID (non-SDT) or base address (SDT) lookup

briefdescription.para: Setup interrupt system for the entire application.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the interrupt controller and sets up the interrupt system for the entire video processing application. It configures the main system interrupt controller (GIC for ARM-based systems).

Initialization steps:
Lookup interrupt controller configuration
Initialize the interrupt controller with configuration
Initialize exception handling system
Register interrupt controller handler with exception table

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, error code otherwise
Uses static count to ensure initialization happens only once
Supports both SDT and non-SDT build systems
Critical for frame buffer writer interrupt handling
